metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:wait_turn [record reshape: turn 1 → long ~500-word generation so follow-up lands mid-turn]

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

## agent_supports = yes

Mistral Vibe has a FIRST-CLASS mid-turn message queue, not an incidental buffer. The TUI (`vibe/cli/textual_ui/`) wires a `QueueController`/`MessageQueue` (app.py:478, message_queue.py). On input submit while the agent is running, `_is_busy()` routes the text through `_handle_queue_submit` → `_enqueue_prompt_with_resources` → `enqueue_prompt` (app.py:894-896, 945-976). Plain-text follow-ups queue; only slash commands and teleport are rejected from the queue (app.py:948-952). So typing a normal follow-up mid-turn is the supported, documented queue path.

## daemon_capability = full

The load-bearing question for this scenario is WHEN the queued message reaches `messages.jsonl`: immediately (mid-turn, which would let the parser's `ClearToolNames` reset open tool state prematurely) or only on dequeue (after turn_done). The source answers definitively: **only on dequeue.**

- `enqueue_prompt` (message_queue.py:207-223) appends the item to the in-memory `_queue` and mounts a `UserMessage(pending=True)` widget. It writes NOTHING to disk — the queued text lives only in memory + the TUI.
- `start_drain_if_needed` (message_queue.py:277-289) explicitly returns early while `agent_running()` is true, so the drain cannot fire mid-turn.
- The drain (`_run_tail_prompt`, message_queue.py:406-416) calls `start_agent_turn(...)`, which runs the queued prompt as a REAL agent turn — that agent-loop turn is what appends the user message and persists it (agent_loop.py:1034 `self.messages.append`, loop.py `_persist` → session_logger.py `messages.jsonl`).

Consequence for irrlicht's vibe parser (core/adapters/inbound/agents/vibe/parser.go): during turn 1, no synthetic user-line ever appears in the transcript, so turn 1's working span is a single contiguous `working` run — no `→ ready` flicker when the follow-up is typed. When the queued message IS finally written (at the queued turn's start, after turn 1's `turn_done`), the parser's `user` case sets `ClearToolNames=true` — but that fires at a LEGITIMATE turn boundary, with turn 1's tools already resolved by its terminal (no-tool_calls) assistant message, so nothing is reset prematurely. Exactly the regression this scenario guards against (queued text as a synthetic mid-turn user-line) is structurally impossible in vibe because the queue is in-memory. daemon=full.

One behavioral note vs claudecode (a caveat, NOT a downgrade): vibe's drain is gated on `agent_running()`, so it ALWAYS consumes the queued message as a distinct SUBSEQUENT turn — never folded into the current turn. The observed arc is therefore working(turn1) → ready(turn1 done) → working(queued turn) → ready(final). The `ready` between the two turns is turn 1's legitimate completion, not a flicker caused by the queued input landing; the queued input landing itself drives ready→working. The scenario's acceptance (working through the queued arrival, no `→ ready` flicker WHEN the queued input lands, cycle ends at ready) is met. This is vibe's queue architecture, faithfully observed — not a daemon defect.

## driver_capability = gap:wait_turn

The recipe elicits the queue by a send-while-working: send the slow prompt, `sleep` to land mid-turn-1, then `send` the plain-text follow-up with NO `wait_turn` between the two sends (a `wait_turn` there would let turn 1 finish and make the follow-up a normal sequential turn, not a queued one). The scaffold's `send` and `sleep` seams are real, so the queuing action itself needs no missing primitive. But to reliably observe turn 1's completion AND the queued turn's completion (and hold the process across both), the recipe needs `wait_turn` — which the freshly-scaffolded vibe driver stubs (`not_implemented`; DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"). This is the same baseline column gap every other vibe turn-based cell carries (2-1, 2-4, 2-5, 1-8): record ports SEAMs 1/2/3 (launch/wait_turn/transcript-resolve) before driving. gap:wait_turn — tooling, not an observability limit.
caveats: The mid-turn follow-up must be PLAIN TEXT, not a slash command: vibe rejects slash commands and teleport from the queue (app.py:948-952). A plain `send` (text + Enter) while busy queues correctly via `_is_busy` → `_handle_queue_submit` → `enqueue_prompt`.

There must be NO wait_turn between the first prompt send and the follow-up send — the follow-up must be typed WHILE turn 1 is still running, otherwise it becomes a normal sequential second turn rather than a queued one. The `sleep` between the two sends is the mid-turn dwell.

Unlike claudecode (which may fold the queued text into the current turn), vibe's drain is gated on `agent_running()` and ALWAYS consumes the queued message as a separate subsequent turn, so a `ready` necessarily appears between turn 1 and the queued turn. The spec models this explicitly (turn1_settle → queued_turn_start → queued_turn_end); it is vibe's design, correctly observed, not a flicker.

PROVISIONAL on live emission ordering: the source proves the queue is in-memory and drained post-turn, but the exact interleaving of turn 1's terminal-assistant write, the sidecar refresh, and the queued turn's first user-line write is only settled by a recording. Confidence 0.8 accordingly.

RECORDING FINDING (2026-07-07-22-26-37_irrlichd-0.5.5+8929eb7.dirty): queued_window is OBSERVABLE and PASSES — turn 1 (bash tool) held working 3570ms while the follow-up was typed and held in vibe message_queue (never written to messages.jsonl until dequeued), with no ready/waiting flicker. BUT the DISTINCT queued turn is NOT observable: vibe drains the queue SYNCHRONOUSLY the instant agent_running() clears, so the queued turn (user 6x7 -> assistant 42, a genuine distinct turn in the transcript) runs with NO ready gap for the daemon to catch. The daemon debounce-coalesces turn 1 + the queued turn into ONE working->ready span (single ready at 22:26:58.529), so queued_turn_start/queued_turn_end find no distinct second working->ready. The queued turn IS distinct at the agent level (transcript proves it) but the daemon under-observes it -> daemon=bug, known_failing, see issue #988.

sources.note: Feature: Mid-turn user message queued — asserts state stays working through the queued arrival, no →ready flicker when the queued input lands, cycle ends at ready.

enqueue_prompt (L207-223) only appends to the in-memory _queue + mounts a pending widget (no disk write); start_drain_if_needed (L277-289) returns early while agent_running(); _run_tail_prompt (L406-416) calls start_agent_turn — so the queued message is persisted only on dequeue, after turn 1 completes.

_is_busy → _handle_queue_submit → _enqueue_prompt_with_resources → enqueue_prompt (L894-976); slash commands + teleport rejected from the queue (L948-952); drain re-triggered after a turn via notify_busy_changed + start_drain_if_needed (L1508-1510).

The `user` case sets ClearToolNames=true (L50-55); assistant-with-tool_calls stays working (L67-71); terminal no-tool_calls assistant → turn_done (L72-75). Because the queued line never appears mid-turn, turn 1's working span is contiguous and ClearToolNames fires only at the legitimate queued-turn boundary.
Freshly scaffolded: DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep" (L113); wait_turn is the stubbed SEAM 2 (not_implemented, L168-170). Recipe needs wait_turn → gap:wait_turn, the baseline column gap record ports.

preconditions: vibe CLI on PATH (~/.local/bin/vibe, v2.19.0) and authenticated (Mistral API key)
irrlichd is recording (run-cell.sh isolated mode, or --attach against a --record daemon)
driver seam ported by record: SEAM 2 wait_turn poll (DRIVE_ELICITS currently lacks wait_turn — gap:wait_turn); SEAMs 1/3 (launch + transcript-path resolve) as well
setup: Create a fresh empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ so the session's working_directory is unique
Vibe mints its own session_<ts>_<hash> id on launch; the daemon session_id is that directory name (single-birth)
The first prompt must keep vibe working several seconds so the follow-up can be typed mid-turn; a slow count-to-10 with pauses suffices

RESHAPE (record): turn 1 changed from a count-to-10 (LLMs do not honor the per-line pause, so the turn finished in <4s and the follow-up landed post-turn) to a ~500-word generation that reliably keeps the turn WORKING for many seconds, so the mid-turn follow-up (typed at +3s) lands while agent_running() and is held in vibe message_queue. Pure text, no tool — observation shape unchanged.

script.text: Use the bash tool to run exactly this command and nothing else: sleep 8 — then reply with just the word done
also, once you have finished, tell me what 6 times 7 is

verify: events.jsonl shows the session entering ready, then exactly one ready→working transition for the initial prompt (turn 1)

events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to ready and NO state_transition to waiting during turn 1's working span while the follow-up is typed and queued (the queued text is not written to messages.jsonl until dequeued)
events.jsonl shows turn 1 settle to ready (terminal text-only assistant), THEN a second ready→working transition as the queued message is dequeued and run as its own turn
events.jsonl eventually shows a working→ready transition for the queued turn; final session state is ready
messages.jsonl contains a SECOND user line (the queued follow-up) appearing only after turn 1's terminal assistant message — never interleaved mid-turn
